2026-04-21T05:44:42.5739049Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-21T05:44:42.5739381Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-21T05:44:42.5739677Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-21T05:44:42.5739921Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-21T05:44:42.5740109Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-21T05:44:42.5740237Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-21T05:44:42.5740385Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-21T05:44:42.5740519Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-21T05:44:42.5740702Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-21T05:44:42.5741080Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-21T05:44:42.5741291Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). At least one label must be provided.","items":{"type":"string"},"minItems":1,"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"remove_labels"},{"description":"Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. This push introduced the package files for the first time into the repository, including `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, generated sources, and tests.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **\\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; can be a policy assignment, role assignment, or ARM template.\\n- **Published Blueprint** (`PublishedBlueprintResource`) — an immutable snapshot of a blueprint at a specific version.\\n- **Assignment** (`AssignmentResource`) — the application of a published blueprint to a scope (subscription or management group).\\n- **Assignment Operation** (`AssignmentOperationResource`) — tracks the status of an in-progress or completed assignment deployment.\\n```\\n\\nReplace the hollow `## Examples` section with a snippet placeholder:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create a blueprint\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Publish a blueprint version\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Create a blueprint assignment\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/blueprint/Azure.ResourceManager.Blueprint/README.md` — edit the \\u0026#34;Authenticate the Client\\u0026#34; section\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Authenticate the client\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate the file `sdk/blueprint/Azure.ResourceManager.Blueprint/tests/Samples/Sample1_Blueprint.cs`:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. $\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\\u0026#34;;\\n            var blueprintCollection = client.GetBlueprints(new ResourceIdentifier(scope));\\n\\n            var blueprintData = new      var operation = await blueprintCollection.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                Azure.WaitUntil.Completed,\\n                blueprintName,\\n                blueprintData);\\n\\n            BlueprintResource blueprint = operation.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created blueprint: {blueprint.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n publishedCollection = blueprint.GetPublishedBlueprints();\\n            var publishedData = new PublishedBlueprintData();\\n\\n            var operation = await publishedCollection.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                Azure.WaitUntil.Completed,\\n                version,\\n                publishedData);\\n\\n            PublishedBlueprintResource published = operation.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Published blueprint version: {published.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n \\u0026#34;myAssignment\\u0026#34;;\\n            string blueprintId =\\n can be a policy assignment, role assignment, or ARM template.\\n- **Published Blueprint** (`PublishedBlueprintResource`) — an immutable snapshot of a blueprint at a specific version.\\n- **Assignment** (`AssignmentResource`) — the application of a published blueprint to a scope (subscription or management group).\\n- **Assignment Operation** (`AssignmentOperationResource`) — tracks the status of an in-progress or completed assignment deployment.\\n```\\n\\nReplace the hollow `## Examples` section with a snippet placeholder:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create a blueprint\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Publish a blueprint version\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Create a blueprint assignment\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/blueprint/Azure.ResourceManager.Blueprint/README.md` — edit the \\u0026#34;Authenticate the Client\\u0026#34; section\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Authenticate the client\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate the file `sdk/blueprint/Azure.ResourceManager.Blueprint/tests/Samples/Sample1_Blueprint.cs`:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Blueprint;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Blueprint.Models;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.Blueprint.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_Blueprint\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task AuthenticateClient()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:Blueprint_AuthenticateClient\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateBlueprint()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:Blueprint_CreateBlueprint\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string blueprintName = \\u0026#34;myBlueprint\\u0026#34;;\\n\\n            // Blueprints can be scoped to a subscription or management group.\\n            // Here we use a subscription scope.\\n            var scope = $\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\\u0026#34;;\\n            var blueprintCollection = client.GetBlueprints(new ResourceIdentifier(scope));\\n\\n            var blueprintData = new BlueprintData\\n            {\\n                Description = \\u0026#34;My sample blueprint\\u0026#34;,\\n                TargetScope = BlueprintTargetScope.Subscription,\\n            };\\n\\n            var operation = await blueprintCollection.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                Azure.WaitUntil.Completed,\\n                blueprintName,\\n                blueprintData);\\n\\n            BlueprintResource blueprint = operation.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created blueprint: {blueprint.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task PublishBlueprint()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:Blueprint_PublishBlueprint\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string blueprintName = \\u0026#34;myBlueprint\\u0026#34;;\\n            string version = \\u0026#34;v1\\u0026#34;;\\n\\n            var scope = $\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\\u0026#34;;\\n            var blueprintCollection = client.GetBlueprints(new ResourceIdentifier(scope));\\n            BlueprintResource blueprint = await blueprintCollection.GetAsync(blueprintName);\\n\\n            var publishedCollection = blueprint.GetPublishedBlueprints();\\n            var publishedData = new PublishedBlueprintData();\\n\\n            var operation = await publishedCollection.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                Azure.WaitUntil.Completed,\\n                version,\\n                publishedData);\\n\\n            PublishedBlueprintResource published = operation.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Published blueprint version: {published.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateAssignment()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:Blueprint_CreateAssignment\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string assignmentName = \\u0026#34;myAssignment\\u0026#34;;\\n            string blueprintId =\\n                $\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/providers/Microsoft.Blueprint/blueprints/myBlueprint/versions/v1\\u0026#34;;\\n\\n            var scope = $\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\\u0026#34;;\\n            var assignmentCollection = client.GetAssignments(new ResourceIdentifier(scope));\\n\\n            var assignmentData = new AssignmentData(\\n                new AssignmentIdentity(AssignmentIdentityType.SystemAssigned),\\n                Azure.Core.AzureLocation.EastUS,\\n                blueprintId,\\n                new System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary(),\\n                new System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary());\\n\\n            var operation = await assignmentCollection.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                Azure.WaitUntil.Completed,\\n                assignmentName,\\n                assignmentData);\\n\\n            AssignmentResource assignment = operation.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created assignment: {assignment.Data.Name}, Status: {assignment.Data.ProvisioningState}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nFor each snippet:\\n- `Blueprint_AuthenticateClient` → README section \\u0026#34;Authenticate the client\\u0026#34;\\n- `Blueprint_CreateBlueprint` → README section \\u0026#34;Examples \\u0026gt; Create a blueprint\\u0026#34;\\n- `Blueprint_PublishBlueprint` → README section \\u0026#34;Examples \\u0026gt; Publish a blueprint version\\u0026#34;\\n- `Blueprint_CreateAssignment` → README section
